title: anisopappus chinensis subsp. lobatus
intro: Anisopappus chinensis subsp. lobatus, also known as the Lobed Anisopappus, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the mountainous regions of southern China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and terpenoids. The Lobed Anisopappus is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in the production of essential oils and tinctures for topical application.
